meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Pricing to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop building tutorials and start shipping revenue. Learn to implement SaaS pricing models using Antigravity and Claude Code in this expert-led guide.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop consuming and start commercializing. By integrating a lean pricing engine directly into your MVP using Antigravity and Claude Code, you force yourself to validate value. Stop building features for free; start shipping a paid solution that solves a real market problem today.
content.intro:
You are drowning in a sea of 'To-Do list' apps and 'weather dashboard' clones. You have spent months watching videos, yet your bank account remains static. This isn't a lack of coding skill; it is a lack of commercial alignment. Tutorial hell persists because you are building for practice, not for profit.

Today, we pivot. We are moving from 'coder' to 'founder' by treating your codebase as a revenue-generating asset. By automating your pricing architecture, you force the market to tell you what they are willing to pay. We will use Antigravity to scaffold the logic and Claude Code to execute the deployment, turning your MVP into a professional SaaS product.

steps.title: Define Your Value Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the unit of value your customer pays for. Is it per seat, per API call, or per project?
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS architect. Generate a pricing strategy document based on a value-based pricing model for a B2B productivity tool.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'create pricing_strategy.md based on value-based metrics'

steps.title: Architect the Pricing Schema
steps.description: Define the database structure for your plans and subscription tiers to ensure scalability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for a multi-tier SaaS pricing model including plans, features, and user limits.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'generate prisma schema for subscription plans'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Billing Logic
steps.description: Use Claude Code to scaffold the backend integration for Stripe checkout sessions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a TypeScript service to handle Stripe Checkout session creation for monthly and yearly tiers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'implement stripe_service.ts with checkout session logic'

steps.title: Implement Webhook Handlers
steps.description: Ensure your app reacts to subscription changes automatically to avoid manual billing management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an Express webhook handler to process Stripe subscription events and update user account status.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'create webhook_controller.ts for stripe events'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Deploy your pricing-enabled MVP to a staging environment for immediate user feedback.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment script for Vercel that includes environment variable configuration for Stripe keys.

pro_tips: Always charge from day one; if users won't pay for your MVP, they won't pay for your 'finished' product.
Use 'feature flagging' to hide premium tools behind your pricing logic, allowing you to iterate without breaking the UX.
Ignore complex multi-currency support until you have your first 10 paying customers in your home currency.
Implement a 'Contact Us for Enterprise' button even if you don't have an enterprise plan; it validates high-intent leads.
Track 'time-to-first-revenue' as your primary KPI, not lines of code or feature count.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live, shift your focus entirely to distribution. Reach out to 50 potential customers on LinkedIn or X and ask them to pay for your MVP. If they refuse, iterate on the value proposition, not the code.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the architecture to turn code into cash. Stop practicing and start building a business that scales. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
